{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing nip-point guards on conveyor system creating entanglement/entrapment hazard at pulleys, rollers, and belt interfaces",
    "Conveyor system non-compliant with OSHA 1910.212 requirements for machine guarding of point-of-operation and nip points",
    "Active work in progress around unguarded rotating conveyor components exposes workers to immediate amputation and crushing injury risk"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop all work and de-energize the conveyor system; apply lockout/tagout",
    "Install engineered nip-point guards at all pulleys, rollers, head/tail terminals, and belt transfer points before resuming any work",
    "Verify guard design and installation meets CEMA and OSHA 1910.212 requirements before re-energizing",
    "Inspect entire conveyor run for additional missing or inadequate guarding and correct all deficiencies",
    "Conduct pre-startup safety verification sign-off confirming all guards are installed and secured"
  ],
  "rationale": "Missing nip-point guards on an active conveyor system is a critical safety hazard. Nip points at pulleys, rollers, and belt interfaces can cause severe entanglement, crushing, and amputation injuries. OSHA 1910.212 explicitly requires guarding of machine point-of-operation and nip points. CEMA standards provide conveyor-specific guarding requirements. Work must stop immediately and guards must be installed before any further activity proceeds.",
  "references": ["OSHA 1910.212", "CEMA"]
}
